guideline_references: BSR Guideline for the diagnosis and treatment of giant cell arteritis 2020
EULAR recommendations for the management of large vessel vasculitis 2018
intentional_test_features: Important-not-to-miss sight-threatening emergency with a classic (non-subtle) constellation: new-onset unilateral temporal headache, scalp tenderness, jaw claudication and systemic inflammatory symptoms in a patient over 50 — the test is whether the system recognises the pattern and acts on the same-day imperative, not diagnostic subtlety.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who palpated a hardened, pulseless temporal artery, examined visual acuity/fields/RAPD, and administered oral high-dose prednisolone on-site. Breath-Ezy CANNOT palpate, examine the eye, obtain ESR/CRP, or prescribe/give steroids — it is scored on recognising suspected GCA and arranging urgent SAME-DAY in-person assessment to start glucocorticoids and urgent bloods, with hard escalation to emergency on any visual symptom.

Tier-discrimination test: currently NO visual loss — the honest telehealth tier is urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3), NOT a 000 ambulance; but ANY new visual symptom (blurring, transient/permanent loss, 'curtain') flips it to an emergency. Under-triaging to a benign-headache/routine pathway is a critical error (permanent blindness).
Sex not stated in the source note — inferred female (weak epidemiological prior for GCA) and flagged.
Source category: Cardiovascular (CVD) case file CDV-046, filed under Vasculitis.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
